meta.title: The Cow and the Tiger — Set 3
meta.subject: English Reading
meta.description: Sequence and character quiz for 'The Cow and the Tiger.'

questions.question: What happened FIRST in the story?
options: The tiger let Bala go
Bala came back to the tiger
Bala grazed in the forest and was caught by the tiger
The tiger promised to protect Bala
questions.explanation: First, Bala went to graze in the shadowy forest, and then an angry tiger caught her. This is the beginning of the story.

questions.question: Bala fed her calf BEFORE the tiger caught her.
questions.explanation: The tiger caught Bala BEFORE she could feed her calf. She asked the tiger to let her go so she COULD go feed her calf.

questions.question: What did Bala do AFTER the tiger agreed to let her go?
options: She ran away and never came back
She went home, fed her calf, and then returned to the forest
She called for help from other cows
She hid in the forest
questions.explanation: After the tiger let her go, 'Bala went back to her grassy home. She quickly fed her calf. Then she returned to the shadowy forest.'

questions.question: Bala had a young animal called a _______.
questions.explanation: A 'calf' is a baby cow. Bala wanted to go home to feed her calf — her baby cow.

questions.question: How did the tiger feel at the START of the story?
options: Happy
Sad
Angry
Surprised
questions.explanation: At the start, the tiger was 'angry.' He caught Bala when she came to graze in the forest.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a sentence about what Bala did:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'She quickly fed her calf.' After the tiger let her go, Bala hurried home and fed her baby.

questions.question: The tiger was happy to see Bala return because he was still hungry.
questions.explanation: The tiger was 'pleased' (happy/impressed) because Bala kept her promise — not because he was hungry. He was amazed by her honesty.

questions.question: Which word BEST describes Bala in this story?
options: Tricky and sneaky
Honest and brave
Lazy and slow
Angry and fierce
questions.explanation: Bala was 'honest' — she told the truth and kept her promise. She was 'brave' — she went back to the tiger even though it was scary.

questions.question: Sort these events — did they happen at the BEGINNING, MIDDLE, or END of the story?
items: Tiger agreed to let Bala go

categories.label: Middle
answers: Tiger agreed to let Bala go

questions.explanation: Beginning: the tiger catches Bala. Middle: the tiger lets her go and she returns. End: the tiger promises to protect Bala.

questions.question: How did the tiger CHANGE from the beginning to the end of the story?
options: He became more angry and dangerous
He changed from angry to grateful and protective
He stayed angry the whole time
He became sad and lonely
questions.explanation: At the beginning, the tiger was 'angry.' At the end, he was pleased, forgiving, and even promised to protect Bala. He changed completely because of her honesty.

questions.question: The tiger was _______ to see Bala — he did not expect her to come back.
questions.hint: Shocked and amazed by something unexpected
questions.explanation: 'Surprised' means shocked by something unexpected. The tiger didn't expect Bala to keep her promise, so he was very surprised when she returned.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form the tiger's final promise:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'Tiger promised to protect Bala.' At the end, the tiger became Bala's protector because she kept her promise.

questions.question: Bala was brave because it was scary to go back to the tiger, but she did it anyway.
questions.explanation: Returning to the tiger was very dangerous and scary, but Bala did it because she had made a promise. This shows true bravery and honesty.

questions.question: Why did the tiger decide NOT to eat Bala?
options: He was not hungry anymore
Bala was too big to eat
He was so impressed by Bala's honesty that he changed his mind
Other tigers told him not to
questions.explanation: The tiger said 'You told the truth. Now I won't attack.' He was so impressed by Bala's courage and honesty in keeping her promise that he changed his heart.

questions.question: Sort these traits — do they describe BALA (the cow) or TIGER?
questions.explanation: Bala showed honesty, kept her promise, and was brave. The tiger was surprised by her return and became protective of her in the end.
